maxContractTerm.value: 10 years (statutory cap). Minn. Stat. § 123B.52, subd. 3 expressly applies 'notwithstanding the provisions of subdivision 1 or section 471.345' and provides: 'The term of such contracts must not exceed ten years.' This 10-year cap is specific to contracts for the transportation of school children (and for petroleum heating fuel or fuel for vehicles) and overrides both the general 'two years with an option ... to renew for an additional two years' cap in subd. 1 and the Uniform Municipal Contracting Law (§ 471.345).
maxContractTerm.citation: Minn. Stat. § 123B.52, subd. 3 (Transportation; fuel) — 'The term of such contracts must not exceed ten years.' (Minnesota Statutes 2025; section history line ends '1Sp2025 c 10 art 2 s 10', which added subd. 6a and did not amend subd. 3.)

rebidRequired.value: Conditional — at expiration of the awarded term, the district must run a fresh competitive-procurement process under Minn. Stat. § 123B.52, subd. 3, which gives the board three permitted methods: (1) direct negotiation by obtaining two or more written quotations 'when possible' (with at least 30 days' published notice requesting quotations before award, and all quotations kept on file at least one year and treated as public information); (2) two or more written quotations for the service; or (3) sealed bids (which must conform to the subd. 1 advertising/award procedure except as otherwise provided in subd. 3). Subdivision 3 begins 'Notwithstanding the provisions of subdivision 1 or section 471.345,' so the general competitive-bidding floor in § 471.345 (which otherwise covers school districts as 'municipalities') does not impose a more restrictive requirement on a transportation contract. The 2024 OSA Legal Compliance Audit Guide for School Districts confirms this framework in Part II.D.6.b ('Contracts for transportation/fuel').
rebidRequired.citation: Minn. Stat. § 123B.52, subd. 3 — 'Notwithstanding the provisions of subdivision 1 or section 471.345, a contract for the transportation of school children ... may be made by direct negotiation, by obtaining two or more written quotations for the service when possible, or upon sealed bids. At least 30 days before awarding a directly negotiated contract, the district must, by published notice, request quotations for the service to be provided.' See also Minn. Stat. § 471.345, subd. 1 (definition of 'municipality' includes 'school district'); Office of the State Auditor, Minnesota Legal Compliance Audit Guide for School Districts (March 2024), Part II.D.6.b.

renewalMechanics.value: N/A — Minn. Stat. § 123B.52, subd. 3 is silent on automatic renewal, extension options, evergreen rollovers, CPI/index adjustments, or fuel-escalator clauses for transportation contracts. The statute speaks only to (a) the maximum 10-year contract term and (b) the permitted procurement methods (direct negotiation with two-or-more quotations and 30-day published notice; two-or-more written quotations; or sealed bids). The 'renew for an additional two years' language in subdivision 1 governs general standard-requirement price contracts, not transportation, and subdivision 3 expressly displaces subdivision 1 'notwithstanding' that subdivision. Subdivision 1 also permits escalation clauses on standard-requirement price contracts ('Such standard requirement price contracts may contain escalation clauses and may provide for a negotiated price increase or decrease based upon a demonstrable industrywide or regional increase or decrease in the vendor's costs'), but subdivision 3 does not incorporate that mechanism for transportation contracts. Renewal beyond the 10-year cap is not authorized by statute; at term-end the district must reprocure using one of subd. 3's three permitted methods. Practical mid-term price-adjustment mechanics (CPI/fuel escalators) are not addressed in subd. 3.
renewalMechanics.citation: Minn. Stat. § 123B.52, subds. 1, 3 (Minnesota Statutes 2025). Compare § 123B.52, subd. 1 (general 'two years with an option ... to renew for an additional two years' for standard-requirement price contracts and escalation-clause authority) with subd. 3 (transportation: 10-year cap, three procurement methods, silent on renewal). See also § 123B.52, subd. 7 (food-service-management contracts may be 'renewed annually after its initial term for not more than four additional years') — a separate, narrower renewal carve-out that is not extended to transportation.

statutoryCitation.value: Primary: Minn. Stat. § 123B.52, subd. 3 (Transportation; fuel) (Minnesota Statutes 2025). Secondary/related: Minn. Stat. § 123B.52, subd. 1 (general contracts; cross-referenced and expressly displaced by subd. 3); Minn. Stat. § 471.345 (Uniform Municipal Contracting Law — defines 'municipality' to include 'school district' in subd. 1, and is expressly overridden by § 123B.52, subd. 3 for transportation contracts); Minn. Stat. § 123B.88, subd. 3 (school board authority to 'contract for the furnishing of authorized transportation under section 123B.52'). Most recent amendment to § 123B.52: 1Sp2025 c 10 art 2 s 10 (added subd. 6a regarding surplus books; did not amend subds. 1 or 3).

districtVariance.value: Unknown — no Minnesota Attorney General opinion, Office of the State Auditor finding, Minnesota Department of Education bulletin, or MSBA/MASBO guidance was located that documents either (a) divergent statewide interpretation of § 123B.52, subd. 3's 10-year cap or three permitted procurement methods, or (b) explicit confirmation of uniform statewide application. The OSA Minnesota Legal Compliance Audit Guide for School Districts (March 2024), Part II.D.6.b ('Contracts for transportation/fuel'), and the MDE Special Education Funding Reference Guide (Section 26: Procurement) both describe the same statutory rule without flagging interpretive splits, but neither speaks directly to district-level variance. A targeted negative search (AG opinion database, OSA audit findings, MSBA resource library and Transportation Best Practices publication, MDE pupil-transportation guidance, individual district policy manuals) did not surface variance evidence either way.

specialEducation.value: No separate state-law contract-term, rebid, renewal, or procurement rule for special-education / IEP-mandated / McKinney-Vento / foster-care / disability-related student transportation was located. Minn. Stat. § 123B.52, subd. 3 by its terms covers 'a contract for the transportation of school children' — it does not distinguish regular-route from special-route transportation, and no narrower or stricter provision in Minn. Stat. ch. 125A (Special Education and Special Programs) or Minn. Rules ch. 3525 (Special Education) sets a different contract-term, rebid, or renewal regime for SPED transportation. The MDE Special Education Funding Reference Guide, Section 26 (Procurement), expressly directs LEAs to follow § 471.345 and § 123B.52 for procurement and does not establish a separate SPED-transportation procurement track. Federal IDEA / 34 C.F.R. § 300.34(c)(16) treats transportation as a 'related service' that the IEP team may require, but that federal duty-to-transport rule does not displace state contract/procurement law and is therefore relevant to the duty to transport, not to the contracting framework.
specialEducation.citation: Minn. Stat. § 123B.52, subd. 3 (Minnesota Statutes 2025); MDE, Special Education Funding Reference Guide, Section 26 (Procurement) (citing Minn. Stat. §§ 471.345 and 123B.52 as the controlling LEA contracting authorities, without a separate SPED-transportation procurement carve-out); 34 C.F.R. § 300.34(c)(16) (federal IDEA — 'related services' — duty-to-transport context only).

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Max contract term is 10 years under § 123B.52, subd. 3
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A longer-term or evergreen-renewal exception exists somewhere in Minn. Stat. ch. 123B, ch. 471 (cooperative purchasing under § 471.345 subd. 15), or in administrative/agency guidance that would let school transportation contracts run beyond 10 years.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened revisor.mn.gov/statutes/cite/123B.52 (full subd. 1, 3, 6a, 7 text re-read); checked Minn. Stat. § 471.345 cooperative-purchasing subd. 15 for any override of § 123B.52; firecrawl_search for 'Minnesota school transportation contract renewal extension evergreen 123B.52'; reviewed MN House Research 'School Transportation Regulation' (July 2023) PDF; checked 2025 session laws (1Sp2025 c 10 art 2 s 10).
challengeMatrix.result: Hold. § 123B.52 subd. 3 verbatim text reconfirmed: 'The term of such contracts must not exceed ten years.' No longer-term carve-out located. § 471.345 subd. 15 (cooperative purchasing) does not displace § 123B.52 subd. 3 for transportation contracts. 2025 amendment added only subd. 6a (surplus books).

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Rebid required at term-end is 'Conditional' — district must run one of three permitted procurement methods under § 123B.52, subd. 3
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: An exception (renewal without re-procurement, sole-source, emergency procurement, cooperative purchasing under § 471.345, interlocal/piggyback, board waiver, state-agency approval, or small-purchase threshold) lets a district extend or roll over without a fresh § 123B.52 subd. 3 procurement.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-read § 123B.52 subd. 3 verbatim; re-read § 471.345 subd. 1 (definitions), subd. 3 (thresholds), subd. 15 (cooperative purchasing); confirmed 'Notwithstanding the provisions of subdivision 1 or section 471.345' clause; verified the 2024 OSA Legal Compliance Audit Guide for School Districts (03/2024 footer), Part II.D.6.b, which restates the same three-method rule and the 30-day notice + one-year quotation-retention requirement.
challengeMatrix.result: Hold. No statutory exception to § 123B.52 subd. 3 located. The three permitted procurement methods (direct negotiation w/ 2+ quotations and 30-day notice; 2+ written quotations; sealed bids) remain mandatory at term-end. § 471.345 subd. 15 cooperative-purchasing authority does not override § 123B.52 subd. 3 for transportation contracts.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Renewal mechanics for transportation contracts = N/A (statute silent on renewal/extension/CPI/fuel escalator within subd. 3)
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Subdivision 1's '2+2 renewal' or 'escalation clauses' authority might be read into subdivision 3 for transportation, or another provision (§ 123B.52 subd. 7 food-service or another subdivision) might import a renewal/escalator carve-out.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-read subd. 1 (general 2-year + 2-year renewal language and escalation-clause authority) and subd. 7 (food-service contracts — 4-year renewal carve-out) verbatim; confirmed subd. 3's 'Notwithstanding the provisions of subdivision 1' clause expressly displaces subd. 1 for transportation; confirmed subd. 7 carve-out is by its terms limited to food-service-management contracts, not transportation.
challengeMatrix.result: Hold. Subdivision 3 silent on renewal/extension/CPI/fuel-escalator mechanics. Subdivision 1's renewal and escalation language does not extend to transportation contracts. Subdivision 7 food-service renewal carve-out is by terms inapplicable to transportation.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: No separate state-law contract-term/rebid/renewal regime for special-education transportation
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Minn. Stat. ch. 125A (Special Education), Minn. R. ch. 3525, MDE SPED guidance, or DHS IEP-services guidance might set a different (longer/shorter/different procurement) contracting rule for SPED, IEP-mandated, McKinney-Vento, foster-care, or disability-related student transportation.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened MDE Special Education Funding Reference Guide, Section 26 (Procurement) PDF (downloaded and pdftotext extracted) — confirmed it cites § 471.345 and § 123B.52 as the controlling LEA contracting authorities and does NOT establish a separate SPED-transportation contracting carve-out; searched Minn. R. ch. 3525 and Minn. Stat. ch. 125A for any SPED-transportation contract/term/rebid rule; verified Minn. R. ch. 7470 governs only bus safety/operations, not contracting.
challengeMatrix.result: Hold. No separate SPED-transportation contracting regime located. MDE Section 26 expressly directs LEAs to follow § 471.345 and § 123B.52 for procurement.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: District-level interpretation variance is 'Unknown' (no authoritative variance evidence located either way)
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: An MN AG opinion, OSA audit finding, MDE bulletin, MSBA guidance memo, or two or more divergent district policy manuals might document either uniform statewide application or divergent district practice in interpreting the 10-year cap or the three permitted procurement methods.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Searched the MN AG opinion database, OSA audit findings (incl. School Districts Legal Compliance Audit Guide 03/2024), MSBA policy services/resource library (incl. April 2023 MSBA 'Transportation Best Practices' publication), MDE pupil-transportation guidance, Minneapolis Public Schools and Saint Paul Public Schools procurement pages; queries included 'Minnesota school transportation contract variance', 'MSBA transportation contract guidance 123B.52', 'AG opinion school transportation 123B.52', 'Minneapolis OR Saint Paul school district transportation RFP renewal'.
challengeMatrix.result: Hold as 'Unknown'. No source either confirming uniform statewide interpretation or documenting divergent district interpretation of § 123B.52 subd. 3 was located. Negative search path documented.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Field-level confidence remains Medium-Low; overall record remains High because the load-bearing fields (Max Term, Rebid, Renewal, Citation, SPED) are confirmed against verbatim statutory text and OSA audit guidance.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Currentness — § 123B.52 subds. 1 and 3 are unchanged through 2025 session laws
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: The 2025 First Special Session (or a pending 2026 session amendment) might have amended subd. 1 or subd. 3 in a way that changes the 10-year cap or the three permitted procurement methods.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-confirmed the § 123B.52 history line on revisor.mn.gov ends '1Sp2025 c 10 art 2 s 10' and searched the 1Sp2025 c 10 art 2 s 10 amendment language directly; firecrawl_search verified 1Sp2025 c 10 art 2 s 10 added subd. 6a (surplus books) only.
challengeMatrix.result: Hold. 2025 amendment did not touch subds. 1 or 3. No pending 2026 session-law amendment to subd. 3 was located.

sourcesReVerified: Minn. Stat. § 123B.52 (revisor.mn.gov/statutes/cite/123B.52) — subd. 1, 3, 6a, 7 verbatim text and history line including 1Sp2025 c 10 art 2 s 10 confirmed

Minn. Stat. § 471.345 (revisor.mn.gov/statutes/cite/471.345) — subd. 1 'municipality' definition (includes 'school district') and subd. 3 ($175,000 sealed-bid threshold) confirmed; subd. 15 cooperative-purchasing authority does NOT override § 123B.52 subd. 3 for transportation contracts
Minn. Stat. § 123B.88 (revisor.mn.gov/statutes/cite/123B.88) — subd. 3 cross-reference to § 123B.52 confirmed
Minn. R. ch. 7470 (revisor.mn.gov/rules/7470/full) — confirmed operational/safety scope only; no contracting rule

OSA, Minnesota Legal Compliance Audit Guide for School Districts (03/2024) (osa.state.mn.us/media/xxlfskva/2023guideschooldistricts.pdf) — Part II.D.6.b 'Contracts for transportation/fuel' confirms three permitted procurement methods, 30-day published-notice requirement, one-year quotation-retention requirement, and 10-year term cap (page 4-9)

MDE, Special Education Funding Reference Guide, Section 26 (Procurement) (education.mn.gov PROD046766) — confirmed cites § 471.345 and § 123B.52 as the controlling LEA contracting authorities, with NO separate SPED-transportation contracting carve-out (text re-extracted via pdftotext)
1Sp2025 c 10 art 2 s 10 — confirmed amendment added subd. 6a (surplus books) only; did NOT amend subds. 1 or 3
sourcesUnreachable: Older standalone OSA 'Legal Compliance Audit Guide -- Section 4' PDF at auditor.state.mn.us/media/oblbzwpu/legalcompliance_08_section04.pdf — body content extracted but document is the older (11/03-vintage) standalone Section 4. Functionally equivalent content was re-verified in the current (03/2024) consolidated School Districts Audit Guide; no claim is now load-bearing on the older URL

sourceLedger.citation: Minn. Stat. § 123B.52 (2025), particularly subd. 1 (Contracts), subd. 3 (Transportation; fuel), subd. 7 (Food service contracts)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Minnesota Statutes 2025; section history shows most recent amendment at 1Sp2025 c 10 art 2 s 10 (added subd. 6a — surplus books). Subdivisions 1 and 3 were not amended in 2025.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Max term (subd. 3: 'The term of such contracts must not exceed ten years.'); Rebid (subd. 3 three permitted methods: direct negotiation w/ two+ quotations and 30-day published notice; two+ written quotations; sealed bids that conform to subd. 1 procedure except as otherwise provided); Renewal mechanics (subd. 3 silent on renewal; subd. 1's 2+2 renewal and escalation-clause authority apply only to non-transportation 'standard requirement price contracts'); Statutory citation
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'Notwithstanding the provisions of subdivision 1 or section 471.345, a contract for the transportation of school children, or a contract for the purchase of petroleum heating fuel or fuel for vehicles may be made by direct negotiation, by obtaining two or more written quotations for the service when possible, or upon sealed bids. At least 30 days before awarding a directly negotiated contract, the district must, by published notice, request quotations for the service to be provided. All quotations obtained must be kept on file for a period of at least one year after receipt. If a contract is made by direct negotiation, all quotations must be public information. If a contract is made upon sealed bids, the procedure for advertising and awarding bids shall conform to the provisions of subdivision 1 except as otherwise provided in this subdivision. The term of such contracts must not exceed ten years.'

sourceLedger.citation: Minn. Stat. § 471.345 (2025) (Uniform Municipal Contracting Law), subd. 1 (definitions), subd. 3 (contracts over $175,000), subd. 15 (cooperative purchasing)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Minnesota Statutes 2025; section last amended at 2023 c 62 art 3 s 14 (construction manager at risk alternative).
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Statutory citation (definition of 'municipality' includes 'school district', so § 471.345 would otherwise reach school-district transportation contracts but is expressly displaced by § 123B.52, subd. 3); Rebid (default municipal bidding thresholds — overridden for transportation contracts by § 123B.52, subd. 3); Confirmed subd. 15 cooperative-purchasing authority does NOT override § 123B.52 subd. 3 for transportation contracts
sourceLedger.relevantText: Subd. 1: 'municipality' means a county, town, city, school district or other municipal corporation or political subdivision of the state authorized by law to enter into contracts.' Subd. 3 establishes the >$175,000 sealed-bid floor, $25,001–$175,000 sealed-bid-or-two-quotations option, and $25,000-or-less open-market option that otherwise apply to municipal contracts. Subd. 15 permits municipalities to use state cooperative-purchasing contracts and national municipal-alliance arrangements without standard competitive bidding, but does not displace § 123B.52 subd. 3 for transportation contracts.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 1 — Codified state statute (school transportation authority)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Minnesota Statutes 2025; most recent amendment 2022 (to subd. 1).
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Statutory citation — confirms the school board's authority to 'contract for the furnishing of authorized transportation under section 123B.52' (cross-references § 123B.52 as the controlling contracting authority).
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'The board may contract for the furnishing of authorized transportation under section 123B.52 ...'

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 4 — Official state-agency guidance (Office of the State Auditor)
sourceLedger.citation: Minnesota Office of the State Auditor, Minnesota Legal Compliance Audit Guide for School Districts (March 2024), Part II.D.3 (Contract Duration) and Part II.D.6.b (Contracts for transportation/fuel), page 4-9
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: 03/2024 footer on the operative pages (4-9 and 4-10) of the consolidated School Districts audit guide.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Max term (10-year cap restated in audit guide); Rebid (three permitted procurement methods for school transportation under § 123B.52, subd. 3); 30-day published-notice and one-year quotation-retention requirements; confirms food-service 4-year renewal carve-out is separate from transportation
sourceLedger.relevantText: Page 4-9 (Contract Duration): 'Note: Food service contracts in compliance with 7 cfr 210.16 may be renewed annually after their initial term for not more than four years. Contracts for the transportation of school children or fuel for heating or transportation must not exceed ten years.' Part II.D.6.b: 'Contracts for transportation/fuel. A contract for transportation of school children or for the purchase of petroleum heating fuel or fuel for vehicles may be made by direct negotiation by obtaining two or more written quotations when possible or on sealed bids. (1) If a contract was made by direct negotiations, were quotations requested by published notice at least 30 days before the contract was awarded? (2) Were written quotes received and were all quotations kept on file for at least one year?'

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 4 — Official state-agency guidance (Minnesota Department of Education)
sourceLedger.citation: Minnesota Department of Education, Special Education Funding Reference Guide, Section 26 (Special Education Procurement)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Latest released version on MDE document server (PROD046766).
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Special education differentiation (LEAs procuring SPED-related goods and services, including transportation, follow § 471.345 and § 123B.52 — no separate SPED-transportation contract-term/renewal regime); background on dollar thresholds and procurement methods
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'State requirements for municipalities bidding and contracting are specified in Minnesota Statutes, section 471.345 Uniform Municipal Contracting Law. ... Additional LEA bidding requirements and responsibilities for work or labor are specified in Minnesota Statutes, section 123B.52 Contracts.' Also: 'There are two separate but similar laws that apply to LEA contracts. Minnesota Statutes, section 123B.52, establishes bidding requirements rules and applies uniquely to school districts. Minnesota Statutes, section 471.345, also known as the Uniform Municipal Contracting law, applies to municipalities generally.' The guide describes the § 123B.52, subd. 1 two-year + two-year renewal framework for general standard-requirement price contracts but does NOT establish a separate SPED-transportation contracting carve-out.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 2 — State administrative code (operational, not procurement)
sourceLedger.citation: Minn. R. ch. 7470 (School Bus Inspection Certificates and Operation)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Current Minnesota Rules as published by the Revisor.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Negative result — Chapter 7470 governs school-bus safety, inspection, and operational standards (including privately owned/operated buses under contract with a school district) but does NOT establish or modify contract-term, rebid, or renewal rules. Confirms that the operative procurement framework is the statute (§ 123B.52), not the administrative code.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Chapter 7470 governs Type A/B/C/D and multifunctional school activity buses; it addresses inspection certificates and operational requirements for buses owned/operated by school districts or non-publics or 'privately owned and operated under a contract or agreement with a school district or nonpublic school'.

sourceLedger.citation: Minnesota House Research Department, 'School Transportation Regulation' (July 2023)
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Negative result — the publication addresses driver qualifications, training, vehicle standards, passenger restraints, and traffic regulations, but does NOT address contract terms, rebid, or renewal mechanics. Confirms no House Research framing of a different transportation-contracting regime.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Overview: 'This brief outlines various Minnesota Statutes governing school-related transportation. The regulatory structure under state law can be categorized into a few areas: driver qualifications such as driver licensing, background checks, and drug testing; training for both drivers and the pupils being transported; vehicle registration and taxation along with tax exemptions; vehicle and equipment standards...'

queriesThatMattered: Re-fetched Minn. Stat. § 123B.52 verbatim text (subd. 1, 3, 6a, 7) and section history line — confirmed 10-year cap, 'Notwithstanding subdivision 1 or section 471.345' clause, three permitted procurement methods, 30-day notice requirement, and 1Sp2025 c 10 art 2 s 10 (subd. 6a only)
Re-fetched Minn. Stat. § 471.345 — confirmed 'school district' included in 'municipality' definition and confirmed subd. 15 cooperative-purchasing authority does not override § 123B.52 subd. 3
Re-fetched Minn. Stat. § 123B.88 — confirmed subd. 3 cross-references § 123B.52 as the controlling contracting authority
Re-fetched Minn. R. ch. 7470 — confirmed safety/operational scope only; no contracting rule
Downloaded and pdftotext-extracted the OSA Minnesota Legal Compliance Audit Guide for School Districts (03/2024) — confirmed Part II.D.6.b language, 10-year cap, 30-day notice, one-year retention

Downloaded and pdftotext-extracted the MDE Special Education Funding Reference Guide, Section 26 (PROD046766) — confirmed § 471.345 and § 123B.52 as controlling LEA contracting authorities; confirmed NO separate SPED-transportation carve-out
Searched 2025 First Special Session Chapter 10 Article 2 Section 10 — confirmed it added subd. 6a (surplus books) only and did NOT amend subds. 1 or 3
verificationNotes.codeCurrency: Minnesota Statutes 2025 (Minnesota Office of the Revisor of Statutes). Section 123B.52 history line ends '1Sp2025 c 10 art 2 s 10' (added subd. 6a on surplus books); subdivisions 1 and 3 unchanged in 2025.
verificationNotes.adminCodeCurrency: Minnesota Rules — current as published by the Minnesota Office of the Revisor of Statutes. Minn. R. ch. 7470 governs bus safety/operation only; no administrative rule alters the statutory contract-term, rebid, or renewal regime.
verificationNotes.sessionLawCheck: 1Sp2025 c 10 art 2 s 10 added subd. 6a (surplus books) only; did not modify subd. 1 or subd. 3. No pending 2026 session-law amendment to subd. 3 located.
negativeSearchPaths: District-level variance — searched: Office of the State Auditor audit findings, Minnesota Attorney General opinion database, MDE pupil-transportation bulletins, Minnesota School Boards Association policy services and 'Transportation Best Practices' (April 2023), Minneapolis Public Schools and Saint Paul Public Schools procurement pages. Queries included 'Minnesota school transportation contract variance', 'MSBA transportation contract guidance 123B.52', 'Minnesota Attorney General opinion 123B.52', 'Minneapolis OR Saint Paul school district transportation RFP renewal'. No source either confirming uniform statewide interpretation or documenting divergent district interpretation of § 123B.52 subd. 3 was located.

Separate special-education contract-term rule — searched Minn. Stat. ch. 125A (Special Education and Special Programs), Minn. R. ch. 3525 (Special Education), MDE Special Education Funding Reference Guide Section 26 (downloaded full PDF and text-extracted), and IEP-services special-transportation guidance. None sets a different contract-term, rebid, or renewal regime for SPED transportation; all reference § 123B.52 and § 471.345 as the procurement framework.

Renewal / extension / CPI-fuel escalator mechanics within subd. 3 — direct re-read of § 123B.52 subd. 3 verbatim text confirms no provision for automatic renewal, evergreen rollover, mutual-extension option, CPI adjustment, or fuel-escalator beyond the 10-year cap. Escalation-clause language and 2+2 renewal option are in subd. 1 only, and subd. 3 expressly applies notwithstanding subd. 1. Food-service 4-year renewal carve-out in subd. 7 is by terms limited to food-service-management contracts.
Cooperative-purchasing / piggyback override — confirmed Minn. Stat. § 471.345 subd. 15 cooperative-purchasing authority does NOT displace § 123B.52 subd. 3 for transportation contracts.
verificationNotes.conflictChecks: Apparent tension between (a) § 471.345 (school districts are 'municipalities' for procurement) and (b) § 123B.52, subd. 3 (transportation contracts) is resolved on the face of subd. 3, which states 'Notwithstanding the provisions of subdivision 1 or section 471.345 ...'. Subd. 3 controls for transportation contracts. The MDE Special Education Funding Reference Guide narrative about the 'two years with an option ... to renew for an additional two years' rule reflects subd. 1 (general standard-requirement price contracts), not subd. 3 (transportation); the SPED guide does not contradict the 10-year cap because it does not address subd. 3 directly. No authoritative conflict was identified between the statute and the OSA Legal Compliance Audit Guide; the 03/2024 OSA guide expressly restates the 10-year cap and the three permitted procurement methods.
